near_term.quote: So this year, we have a plan to invest around another INR50 crores in capex, okay, in addition to whatever we did previously.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: But now coming to your question, we believe that we can go up to about INR30 billion to INR32 billion with our current capacities and with these incremental investments made this year and next year.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: Then I will -- all the INR2,000 crores or whatever is there in the pending order will be executed this year.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: This year will be one of the highest years for TDPS in Hydro.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: This segment will result in some high-value order wins for TDPS in this quarter and next.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: And I think in about 2 or 3 months from now, I will be happy to answer all these questions in full detail.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

near_term.quote: And this total amount of investment, we will discuss it in about 2 or 3 months.
near_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

medium_term.quote: We've revised our guidance for FY '27 at INR2,400-plus crores with an extremely high probability to increase our guidance further to match the rate of order inflow that we saw in Q4 and what we expect in this financial year from Q1 to Q4.
medium_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

medium_term.quote: And this INR50 crores once again will go towards adding incremental capacity in certain areas, which we consider to be bottlenecks and we'll be investing approximately the same amount next year also in a little bit more automation and again, debottlenecking certain capacities and adding small capacities in certain areas.
medium_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

medium_term.quote: I said that we have a capacity to address INR32 billion for FY '28, yes, but that is where management is also expecting numbers to be on a conservative basis, yes.
medium_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

medium_term.quote: So we are basically covering what we believe we can do as sales for up to FY '28.
medium_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

medium_term.quote: And order book growth, we have not given the guidance for next financial year, which is FY '28 as yet.
medium_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

long_term.quote: Now our turbine customers, engine customers are, on an average, planning to double capacity by 2030, some a little bit more, some a little bit less.
long_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

long_term.quote: And so we'll be looking at the big ramp-up taking place in our large generator business only in calendar '28.
long_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

long_term.quote: Meanwhile, we'll continue to supply with whatever existing capacity that we have, but we have started making commitments to the market on our larger numbers from calendar '28 onwards, which will basically be FY '29.
long_term.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: Order book for the Manufacturing segment is INR19.73 billion, out of which INR16.77 billion is the generator business, INR2.47 billion is railway business and spares and aftermarket business is INR0.2 billion, Turkey is INR0.29 billion.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: And as we have told in the presentation, we are expecting 10% to 12% growth.
unclassified.speaker: Vinay Hegde

unclassified.quote: Now I'll come to the order book, market situation and guidance.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: In general, we see a very buoyant market for TDPS in all segments of the business.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: With all sectors in full force, we see a very strong order inflow situation continuing, and the focus right now is on execution.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: However, we put that behind us, and now we are heavily focused on Q1 execution, where based on the progress so far, we see that we will do better than Q4 of last financial year.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: We are now separating manufacturing lines, which will improve focus and execution.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: We expect the Motor segment to stabilize and we see improved traction going forward.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: Finally, I'll come to the guidance.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: In general, what I can say right now, and that also alludes to the comments that I made in the investor presentation is that we are now putting a lot of focus on the large generator business, where we see our projections.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: We're focusing on larger AI data centers where we see a lot of demand now in the future, it will shift to the larger sizes because definitely it's more efficient.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: So we don't expect this to happen once again.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: Yes, and you can expect gross contribution levels to move back to the average historical levels.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: This is not including the investment that we will have to make for the larger generators.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: That will be taken as a separate piece.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: For the next 2, 3 quarters, I don't see any major disruption to our margin guidance.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: But I will give the -- we're seeing a growth in our quarter-on-quarter inflow numbers.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar

unclassified.quote: Overall, I expect us to be fairly neutral, and I don't expect a big impact on the margins.
unclassified.speaker: Nikhil Kumar
